title: Primitive Types 5
description: Tuples can hold multiple values of different types. You can destructure them to access individual elements.
instructions: Destructure the tuple to extract the values into individual variables.
hints: Tuple destructuring uses the same parenthesized pattern as tuple creation.
The syntax is: let (var1, var2) = tuple;
explanation: Tuples can be destructured using pattern matching in a `let` binding. The pattern `(name, age)` matches the tuple structure and binds each element to the corresponding variable. This is a common Rust idiom for working with tuples and is also used extensively in match expressions.
